machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane T4SS channel component (VirB3/VirB4 energetic module)
machinery_genes.quote: Three of the remaining genes (virB3, virB4, and virB6) of both A. phagocytophila and E. chaffeensis were arranged downstream from a sodB gene and cotranscribed with the sodB gene through one or more sodB promoters in human leukocytes.
machinery_genes.note: Founding apparatus paper (Ohashi et al 2002) identifies virB3 in E. chaffeensis as one of the eight virB/virD type IV secretion machinery genes, located in the sodB-proximal locus.

machinery_genes.quote: Three of the remaining genes (virB3, virB4, and virB6) of both A. phagocytophila and E. chaffeensis were arranged downstream from a sodB gene and cotranscribed with the sodB gene through one or more sodB promoters in human leukocytes.
machinery_genes.note: VirB4 is the conserved T4SS ATPase. Named in E. chaffeensis by Ohashi et al 2002 in the sodB-virB3-virB4-virB6 locus.

machinery_genes.quote: Three of the remaining genes (virB3, virB4, and virB6) of both A. phagocytophila and E. chaffeensis were arranged downstream from a sodB gene and cotranscribed with the sodB gene through one or more sodB promoters in human leukocytes.
machinery_genes.note: Single virB6 named in Ohashi 2002; later resolved into four tandem paralogs (virB6-1 to -4) by Bao et al 2008 (PMID 18952796), each listed separately below.

machinery_genes.role: polytopic inner-membrane channel component (VirB6 paralog; interacts with VirB9)
machinery_genes.quote: despite its small genome size, E. chaffeensis has four tandem virB6 paralogs (virB6-1, -2, -3, and -4) that are 3- to 10-fold larger than A. tumefaciens virB6
machinery_genes.note: Bao et al 2008. Coimmunoprecipitation showed VirB9 interaction with VirB6-1 and VirB6-2.

machinery_genes.role: polytopic inner-membrane channel component (VirB6 paralog; interacts with VirB9)
machinery_genes.quote: despite its small genome size, E. chaffeensis has four tandem virB6 paralogs (virB6-1, -2, -3, and -4) that are 3- to 10-fold larger than A. tumefaciens virB6
machinery_genes.note: Bao et al 2008. An 80-kDa fragment of VirB6-2 was enriched in E. chaffeensis-containing vacuoles and interacts with VirB6-3 and VirB6-4.

machinery_genes.quote: despite its small genome size, E. chaffeensis has four tandem virB6 paralogs (virB6-1, -2, -3, and -4) that are 3- to 10-fold larger than A. tumefaciens virB6
machinery_genes.note: Bao et al 2008. VirB6-4 interacts with VirB6-1, VirB6-2, and VirB6-3.

machinery_genes.quote: despite its small genome size, E. chaffeensis has four tandem virB6 paralogs (virB6-1, -2, -3, and -4) that are 3- to 10-fold larger than A. tumefaciens virB6
machinery_genes.note: Bao et al 2008. Part of the VirB6 quadruple-paralog subassembly cooperating in type IV secretion.

machinery_genes.quote: Five of the genes (virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, and virD4) were located downstream from a ribA gene.
machinery_genes.note: Ohashi et al 2002; virB8 heads the ribA-proximal polycistronic locus (virB8-virB9-virB10-virB11-virD4) controlled by tandem promoters upstream of virB8.

machinery_genes.quote: Five of the genes (virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, and virD4) were located downstream from a ribA gene.
machinery_genes.note: Ohashi et al 2002 names virB9 in E. chaffeensis. Bao et al 2008 (PMID 18952796) further shows VirB9 protein is expressed and interacts with the VirB6 paralogs: 'The four VirB6 proteins and VirB9 were expressed by E. chaffeensis in THP-1 cells.'

machinery_genes.quote: Five of the genes (virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, and virD4) were located downstream from a ribA gene.
machinery_genes.note: Ohashi et al 2002; part of the ribA-proximal virB8-D4 operon in E. chaffeensis.

machinery_genes.quote: Five of the genes (virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, and virD4) were located downstream from a ribA gene.
machinery_genes.note: Ohashi et al 2002; VirB11-family ATPase named in the E. chaffeensis virB8-D4 locus.

machinery_genes.role: type IV coupling protein (substrate receptor ATPase)
machinery_genes.quote: Five of the genes (virB8, virB9, virB10, virB11, and virD4) were located downstream from a ribA gene.
machinery_genes.note: Ohashi et al 2002; virD4 is the terminal gene of the ribA-proximal operon. VirD4 is the coupling protein that recruits effectors to the VirB channel.

notes:
TYPE MISMATCH FLAG: the benchmark labels this instance T1SS, but the published literature is unambiguous that the E. chaffeensis 'VirB' system is a Type IV secretion system (T4SS) homologous to the Agrobacterium tumefaciens VirB/VirD4 system, NOT a three-component (ABC/MFP/OMP) T1SS. The supplied T1SS apparatus brief vocabulary (HlyB/HlyD/TolC) does not apply; machinery genes below are the literature-named VirB/VirD4 T4SS components. The mismatch likely originates from the detector under test (this is exactly the kind of call the benchmark is meant to evaluate), so the answer key records the true literature machinery rather than forcing a T1SS interpretation.

Founding apparatus paper: Ohashi, Zhi, Lin & Rikihisa 2002 (Infect Immun) identified and transcriptionally characterized the E. chaffeensis (and A. phagocytophila) T4SS gene clusters. Eight virB/virD genes are split across two loci: a ribA-proximal polycistron (virB8-virB9-virB10-virB11-virD4) and a sodB-proximal cluster (virB3-virB4-virB6). All eight are named verbatim and recorded as core machinery. Bao et al 2008 (J Bacteriol) resolved the single virB6 into four tandem paralogs (virB6-1..-4) and showed protein-level expression and VirB6/VirB9 interaction in E. chaffeensis-containing vacuoles; the four paralogs are listed individually, with the generic VirB6 entry retained to mark the Ohashi naming. Rikihisa 2017 review supports the overall VirB/VirD4 assignment.

Deliberately excluded: the T4SS effectors AnkA, Ats-1, Etf-1/ECH0825 and this instance's curated effectors ECH_0166/ECH_0170 (substrates, not machinery); ribA and sodB (flanking housekeeping genes, not secretion components, only mentioned as operon landmarks); regulators. VirB2 and VirB7 (pilin and lipoprotein of the canonical VirB set) are NOT listed: no E. chaffeensis paper found that names them as components of this system, consistent with the reductive ehrlichial genome and the literature describing only eight virB/virD genes. Status COMPLETE because the core T4SS energetic and channel components (VirB3, VirB4, VirB6 paralogs, VirB8-VirB11, VirD4) are all literature-documented for this organism; the absence of VirB2/VirB7 reflects the published gene complement, not a gap in curation. All quotes verbatim from the cited PubMed abstracts / PMC articles.
